tEn: Jukebox: A Generative Model for Music
tZh: Jukebox：一种生成音乐的生成模型
sEn: Jukebox generates music with singing in raw audio using multi-scale VQ-VAE and autoregressive Transformers.
sZh: Jukebox 使用多尺度 VQ-VAE 和自回归 Transformer 在原始音频域中生成带歌声的音乐。
absEn: We introduce Jukebox, a model that generates music with singing in the raw audio domain. We tackle the long context of raw audio using a multi-scale VQ-VAE to compress it to discrete codes, and modeling those using autoregressive Transformers. We show that the combined model at scale can generate high-fidelity and diverse songs with coherence up to multiple minutes. We can condition on artist and genre to steer the musical and vocal style, and on unaligned lyrics to make the singing more controllable. We are releasing thousands of non cherry-picked samples at https://jukebox.openai.com, along with model weights and code at https://github.com/openai/jukebox
absZh: 我们介绍了 Jukebox，一种在原始音频域中生成带歌声的音乐的模型。我们使用多尺度 VQ-VAE 将原始音频的长上下文压缩为离散编码，并使用自回归 Transformer 对这些编码进行建模。我们展示了该组合模型在规模化后能够生成高保真度且多样化的歌曲，连贯性可达数分钟。我们可以通过艺术家和流派来引导音乐和声乐风格，并通过未对齐的歌词使歌声更可控。我们在 https://jukebox.openai.com 上发布了数千个非精心挑选的样本，并在 https://github.com/openai/jukebox 上发布了模型权重和代码。

contrib.en: Introduces Jukebox, a generative model for music with singing in raw audio domain.
contrib.zh: 提出Jukebox，一种在原始音频域生成带歌声音乐的生成模型。

contrib.en: Uses multi-scale VQ-VAE to compress raw audio into discrete codes.
contrib.zh: 使用多尺度VQ-VAE将原始音频压缩为离散编码。

contrib.en: Models compressed codes with autoregressive Transformers for long-range coherence.
contrib.zh: 用自回归Transformer对压缩编码建模，实现长程连贯性。

contrib.en: Enables conditioning on artist, genre, and unaligned lyrics for controllable generation.
contrib.zh: 支持以艺术家、流派和未对齐歌词为条件，实现可控生成。

contrib.en: Demonstrates high-fidelity and diverse song generation with coherence up to multiple minutes.
contrib.zh: 展示高保真、多样化的歌曲生成，连贯性可达数分钟。

contrib.en: Releases model weights, code, and thousands of samples for reproducibility.
contrib.zh: 发布模型权重、代码及数千个样本，确保可复现性。

limits.en: Generated songs lack traditional large-scale musical structures like repeating choruses.
limits.zh: 生成的歌曲缺乏传统大型音乐结构，如重复的副歌。

limits.en: Audio quality suffers from noise and scratchiness at small time scales.
limits.zh: 音频质量在小时间尺度上存在噪声和刺耳感。

limits.en: Model trained only on English-language songs, limiting language and style diversity.
limits.zh: 模型仅用英语歌曲训练，限制了语言和风格多样性。

limits.en: Slow sampling and upsampling process (hours per minute of audio).
limits.zh: 采样和上采样过程缓慢（每分钟音频需数小时）。

limits.en: Lyrics alignment remains imperfect for fast-paced genres like hip-hop.
limits.zh: 歌词对齐在快节奏流派（如嘻哈）中仍不完美。

items.en: We introduce Jukebox, a model that generates music with singing in the raw audio domain. We tackle the long context of raw audio using a multi-scale VQ-VAE to compress it to discrete codes, and modeling those using autoregressive Transformers. We show that the combined model at scale can generate high-fidelity and diverse songs with coherence up to multiple minutes. We can condition on artist and genre to steer the musical and vocal style, and on unaligned lyrics to make the singing more controllable. We are releasing thousands of non cherry-picked samples, along with model weights and code.
items.zh: 我们提出了Jukebox，一个在原始音频域中生成带有歌声的音乐的模型。我们使用多尺度VQ-VAE将原始音频的长上下文压缩为离散编码，并使用自回归Transformer对这些编码进行建模。我们展示了在规模上，该组合模型能够生成具有长达数分钟连贯性的高保真度和多样化的歌曲。我们可以通过艺术家和流派条件来控制音乐和声乐风格，并通过非对齐的歌词使歌唱更可控。我们发布了数千个非精选样本，以及模型权重和代码。

items.en: Music is an integral part of human culture, existing from the earliest periods of human civilization and evolving into a wide diversity of forms. It evokes a unique human spirit in its creation, and the question of whether computers can ever capture this creative process has fascinated computer scientists for decades. We have had algorithms generating piano sheet music (Hiller Jr & Isaacson, 1957; Moorer, 1972; Hadjeres et al., 2017; Huang et al., 2017), digital vocoders generating a singer’s voice (Bonada & Serra, 2007; Saino et al., 2006; Blaauw & Bonada, 2017) and also synthesizers producing timbres for various musical instruments (Engel et al., 2017, 2019). Each captures a specific aspect of music generation: melody, composition, timbre, and the human voice singing. However, a single system to do it all remains elusive.
items.zh: 音乐是人类文化不可或缺的一部分，从人类文明的最早期就已存在，并演变成多种多样的形式。它在创作中唤起独特的人类精神，而计算机能否捕捉这一创作过程的问题几十年来一直吸引着计算机科学家。我们已经有了生成钢琴乐谱的算法（Hiller Jr & Isaacson, 1957; Moorer, 1972; Hadjeres et al., 2017; Huang et al., 2017）、生成歌手声音的数字声码器（Bonada & Serra, 2007; Saino et al., 2006; Blaauw & Bonada, 2017）以及为各种乐器生成音色的合成器（Engel et al., 2017, 2019）。每一种都捕捉了音乐生成的一个特定方面：旋律、作曲、音色和人类歌声。然而，一个能完成所有任务的单一系统仍然难以实现。

items.en: The field of generative models has made tremendous progress in the last few years. One of the aims of generative modeling is to capture the salient aspects of the data and to generate new instances indistinguishable from the true data The hypothesis is that by learning to produce the data we can learn the best features of the data1. We are surrounded by highly complex distributions in the visual, audio, and text domain, and in recent years we have developed advances in text generation (Radford et al., ), speech generation (Xie et al., 2017) and image generation (Brock et al., 2019; Razavi et al., 2019). The rate of progress in this field has been rapid, where only a few years ago we had algorithms producing blurry faces (Kingma & Welling, 2014; Goodfellow et al., 2014) but now we now can generate high-resolution faces indistinguishable from real ones (Zhang et al., 2019b). ††1Richard Feynmann famously said, “What I cannot create, I do not understand”
items.zh: 生成模型领域在过去几年取得了巨大进展。生成建模的目标之一是捕捉数据的显著方面，并生成与真实数据无法区分的新实例。假设是通过学习生成数据，我们可以学习数据的最佳特征。我们被视觉、音频和文本领域的高度复杂分布所包围，近年来我们在文本生成（Radford et al.）、语音生成（Xie et al., 2017）和图像生成（Brock et al., 2019; Razavi et al., 2019）方面取得了进展。该领域的进步速度很快，就在几年前，我们还有算法生成模糊的人脸（Kingma & Welling, 2014; Goodfellow et al., 2014），但现在我们可以生成与真实人脸无法区分的高分辨率人脸（Zhang et al., 2019b）。††1 理查德·费曼有句名言：“我不能创造的东西，我就不理解。”

items.en: Generative models have been applied to the music generation task too. Earlier models generated music symbolically in the form of a pianoroll, which specifies the timing, pitch, velocity, and instrument of each note to be played. (Yang et al., 2017; Dong et al., 2018; Huang et al., 2019a; Payne, 2019; Roberts et al., 2018; Wu et al., 2019). The symbolic approach makes the modeling problem easier by working on the problem in the lower-dimensional space. However, it constrains the music that can be generated to being a specific sequence of notes and a fixed set of instruments to render with. In parallel, researchers have been pursuing the non-symbolic approach, where they try to produce music directly as a piece of audio. This makes the problem more challenging, as the space of raw audio is extremely high dimensional with a high amount of information content to model. There has been some success, with models producing piano pieces either in the raw audio domain (Oord et al., 2016; Mehri et al., 2017; Yamamoto et al., 2020) or in the spectrogram domain (Vasquez & Lewis, 2019). The key bottleneck is that modeling the raw audio directly introduces extremely long-range dependencies, making it computationally challenging to learn the high-level semantics of music. A way to reduce the difficulty is to learn a lower-dimensional encoding of the audio with the goal of losing the less important information but retaining most of the musical information. This approach has demonstrated some success in generating short instrumental pieces restricted to a set of a few instruments (Oord et al., 2017; Dieleman et al., 2018).
items.zh: 生成模型也已应用于音乐生成任务。早期的模型以钢琴卷帘的形式符号化地生成音乐，指定每个音符的时机、音高、力度和乐器（Yang et al., 2017; Dong et al., 2018; Huang et al., 2019a; Payne, 2019; Roberts et al., 2018; Wu et al., 2019）。符号化方法通过在低维空间中处理问题，使建模问题更容易。然而，它限制了生成的音乐只能是特定的音符序列和固定的乐器集。与此同时，研究人员一直在探索非符号化方法，即尝试直接生成音频片段。这使得问题更具挑战性，因为原始音频空间维度极高，包含大量需要建模的信息。已有一些成功案例，模型在原始音频域（Oord et al., 2016; Mehri et al., 2017; Yamamoto et al., 2020）或频谱图域（Vasquez & Lewis, 2019）生成钢琴曲。关键瓶颈在于直接建模原始音频会引入极长程依赖，使得学习音乐的高级语义在计算上具有挑战性。降低难度的一种方法是学习音频的低维编码，目标是丢失不太重要的信息，但保留大部分音乐信息。这种方法在生成仅限于少数几种乐器的短乐器片段方面取得了一些成功（Oord et al., 2017; Dieleman et al., 2018）。

items.en: In this work, we show that we can use state-of-the-art deep generative models to produce a single system capable of generating diverse high-fidelity music in the raw audio domain, with long-range coherence spanning multiple minutes. Our approach uses a hierarchical VQ-VAE architecture (Razavi et al., 2019) to compress audio into a discrete space, with a loss function designed to retain the maximum amount of musical information, while doing so at increasing levels of compression. We use an autoregressive Sparse Transformer (Child et al., 2019; Vaswani et al., 2017) trained with maximum-likelihood estimation over this compressed space, and also train autoregressive upsamplers to recreate the lost information at each level of compression.
items.zh: 在这项工作中，我们展示了可以使用最先进的深度生成模型来构建一个单一系统，能够在原始音频域中生成多样化的高保真音乐，并具有跨越数分钟的长程连贯性。我们的方法使用分层VQ-VAE架构（Razavi et al., 2019）将音频压缩到离散空间，损失函数设计为保留最大量的音乐信息，同时以越来越高的压缩级别进行。我们使用自回归稀疏Transformer（Child et al., 2019; Vaswani et al., 2017）在这个压缩空间上进行最大似然估计训练，并训练自回归上采样器以在每个压缩级别重建丢失的信息。

items.en: We show that our models can produce songs from highly diverse genres of music like rock, hip-hop, and jazz. They can capture melody, rhythm, long-range composition, and timbres for a wide variety of instruments, as well as the styles and voices of singers to be produced with the music. We can also generate novel completions of existing songs. Our approach allows the option to influence the generation process: by swapping the top prior with a conditional prior, we can condition on lyrics to tell the singer what to sing, or on midi to control the composition. We release our model weights and training and sampling code at https://github.com/openai/jukebox.
items.zh: 我们展示了我们的模型可以生成来自高度多样化音乐流派（如摇滚、嘻哈和爵士）的歌曲。它们可以捕捉旋律、节奏、长程作曲以及各种乐器的音色，以及随音乐产生的歌手风格和声音。我们还可以生成现有歌曲的新颖续写。我们的方法允许影响生成过程：通过将顶部先验替换为条件先验，我们可以对歌词进行条件化以告诉歌手唱什么，或者对MIDI进行条件化以控制作曲。我们在https://github.com/openai/jukebox发布了模型权重以及训练和采样代码。

items.en: We consider music in the raw audio domain represented as a continuous waveform 𝐱∈[−1,1]T𝐱superscript11𝑇\mathbf{x}\in[-1,1]^{T}, where the number of samples T𝑇T is the product of the audio duration and the sampling rate typically ranging from 16 kHz to 48 kHz. For music, CD quality audio, 44.1 kHz samples stored in 16 bit precision, is typically enough to capture the range of frequencies perceptible to humans. As an example, a four-minute-long audio segment will have an input length of ∼10similar-toabsent10{\sim}10 million, where each position can have 16 bits of information. In comparison, a high-resolution RGB image with 1024×1024102410241024\times 1024 pixels has an input length of ∼3similar-toabsent3{\sim}3 million, and each position has 24 bits of information. This makes learning a generative model for music extremely computationally demanding with increasingly longer durations; we have to capture a wide range of musical structures from timbre to global coherence while simultaneously modeling a large amount of diversity.
items.zh: 我们考虑原始音频域中的音乐，表示为连续波形𝐱∈[−1,1]^T，其中样本数T是音频时长与采样率的乘积，通常范围从16 kHz到48 kHz。对于音乐，CD质量音频（44.1 kHz采样，16位精度存储）通常足以捕捉人类可感知的频率范围。例如，一个四分钟的音频片段将有约1000万个输入长度，每个位置可以有16位信息。相比之下，一个1024×1024像素的高分辨率RGB图像有约300万个输入长度，每个位置有24位信息。这使得学习音乐生成模型在计算上极其困难，尤其是随着时长增加；我们必须捕捉从音色到全局连贯性的广泛音乐结构，同时建模大量的多样性。

items.en: To make this task feasible, we use the VQ-VAE (Oord et al., 2017; Dieleman et al., 2018; Razavi et al., 2019) to compress raw audio to a lower-dimensional space. A one-dimensional VQ-VAE learns to encode an input sequence 𝐱=⟨𝐱t⟩t=1T𝐱superscriptsubscriptdelimited-⟨⟩subscript𝐱𝑡𝑡1𝑇\mathbf{x}=\langle\mathbf{x}_{t}\rangle_{t=1}^{T} using a sequence of discrete tokens 𝐳=⟨zs∈[K]⟩s=1S𝐳superscriptsubscriptdelimited-⟨⟩subscript𝑧𝑠delimited-[]𝐾𝑠1𝑆\mathbf{z}=\langle z_{s}\in[K]\rangle_{s=1}^{S}, where K𝐾K denotes the vocabulary size and we call the ratio T/S𝑇𝑆T/S the hop length. It consists of an encoder E​(𝐱)𝐸𝐱E(\mathbf{x}) which encodes 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x} into a sequence of latent vectors 𝐡=⟨𝐡s⟩s=1S𝐡superscriptsubscriptdelimited-⟨⟩subscript𝐡𝑠𝑠1𝑆\mathbf{h}=\langle\mathbf{h}_{s}\rangle_{s=1}^{S}, a bottleneck that quantizes 𝐡s↦𝐞zsmaps-tosubscript𝐡𝑠subscript𝐞subscript𝑧𝑠\mathbf{h}_{s}\mapsto\mathbf{e}_{z_{s}} by mapping each 𝐡ssubscript𝐡𝑠\mathbf{h}_{s} to its nearest vector 𝐞zssubscript𝐞subscript𝑧𝑠\mathbf{e}_{z_{s}} from a codebook C={𝐞k}k=1K𝐶superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝐞𝑘𝑘1𝐾C=\{\mathbf{e}_{k}\}_{k=1}^{K}, and a decoder D​(𝐞)𝐷𝐞D(\mathbf{e}) that decodes the embedding vectors back to the input space. It is thus an auto-encoder with a discretization bottleneck. The VQ-VAE is trained using the following objective:

items.en: where sgsg\mathrm{sg} denotes the stop-gradient operation, which passes zero gradient during backpropagation. The reconstruction loss ℒreconssubscriptℒrecons\mathcal{L}_{\text{recons}} penalizes for the distance between the input 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x} and the reconstructed output 𝐱^=D​(𝐞z)^𝐱𝐷subscript𝐞𝑧\widehat{\mathbf{x}}=D(\mathbf{e}_{z}), and ℒcodebooksubscriptℒcodebook\mathcal{L}_{\text{codebook}} penalizes the codebook for the distance between the encodings 𝐡𝐡\mathbf{h} and their nearest neighbors 𝐞zsubscript𝐞𝑧\mathbf{e}_{z} from the codebook. To stabilize the encoder, we also add ℒcommitsubscriptℒcommit\mathcal{L}_{\text{commit}} to prevent the encodings from fluctuating too much, where the weight β𝛽\beta controls the amount of contribution of this loss. To speed up training, the codebook loss ℒcodebooksubscriptℒcodebook\mathcal{L}_{\text{codebook}} instead uses EMA updates over the codebook variables. Razavi et al. (2019) extends this to a hierarchical model where they train a single encoder and decoder but break up the latent sequence 𝐡𝐡\mathbf{h} into a multi-level representation [𝐡(1),⋯,𝐡(L)]superscript𝐡1⋯superscript𝐡𝐿[\mathbf{h}^{(1)},\cdots,\mathbf{h}^{(L)}] with decreasing sequence lengths, each learning its own codebook C(l)superscript𝐶𝑙C^{(l)}. They use non-autoregressive encoder-decoders and jointly train all levels with a simple mean-squared loss.
items.zh: 其中sg表示停止梯度操作，在反向传播期间传递零梯度。重建损失ℒ_recons惩罚输入𝐱与重建输出𝐱̂=D(𝐞_z)之间的距离，而ℒ_codebook惩罚码本中编码𝐡与其最近邻𝐞_z之间的距离。为了稳定编码器，我们还添加了ℒ_commit以防止编码波动过大，其中权重β控制该损失的贡献量。为了加速训练，码本损失ℒ_codebook改为使用码本变量的EMA更新。Razavi等人（2019）将其扩展到分层模型，其中他们训练单个编码器和解码器，但将潜在序列𝐡分解为多级表示[𝐡^{(1)},⋯,𝐡^{(L)}]，序列长度递减，每个级别学习自己的码本C^{(l)}。他们使用非自回归编码器-解码器，并用简单的均方损失联合训练所有级别。

items.capEn: Figure 1: We first train three separate VQ-VAE models with different temporal resolutions. At each level, the input audio is segmented and encoded into latent vectors 𝐡tsubscript𝐡𝑡\mathbf{h}_{t}, which are then quantized to the closest codebook vectors 𝐞ztsubscript𝐞subscript𝑧𝑡\mathbf{e}_{z_{t}}. The code ztsubscript𝑧𝑡z_{t} is a discrete representation of the audio that we later train our prior on. The decoder takes the sequence of codebook vectors and reconstructs the audio. The top level learns the highest degree of abstraction, since it is encoding longer audio per token while keeping the codebook size the same. Audio can be reconstructed using the codes at any one of the abstraction levels, where the least abstract bottom-level codes result in the highest-quality audio, as shown in Figure 4. For the detailed structure of each component, see Figure 7.
items.capZh: 图1：我们首先训练三个具有不同时间分辨率的独立VQ-VAE模型。在每个级别，输入音频被分段并编码为潜在向量𝐡_t，然后量化到最近的码本向量𝐞_{z_t}。编码z_t是音频的离散表示，我们随后在其上训练先验。解码器接收码本向量序列并重建音频。顶层学习最高程度的抽象，因为它在保持码本大小不变的情况下每个令牌编码更长的音频。音频可以使用任何抽象级别的编码重建，其中抽象程度最低的底层编码产生最高质量的音频，如图4所示。每个组件的详细结构见图7。

items.en: Inspired by the results from the hierarchical VQ-VAE model (Razavi et al., 2019) for images, we consider applying the same technique to model raw audio using three different levels of abstraction, as illustrated in Figure 1. At each level, we use residual networks consisting of WaveNet-style non-causal 1-D dilated convolutions, interleaved with downsampling and upsampling 1-D convolutions to match different hop lengths. A detailed description of the architecture is provided in Appendix B.1. We make a number of modifications to our VQ-VAE compared to the ones in (Oord et al., 2017; Razavi et al., 2019), as described in the following subsections.
items.zh: 受图像分层VQ-VAE模型（Razavi et al., 2019）结果的启发，我们考虑应用相同的技术，使用三个不同抽象级别对原始音频进行建模，如图1所示。在每个级别，我们使用由WaveNet风格的非因果一维扩张卷积组成的残差网络，并与下采样和上采样一维卷积交错以匹配不同的跳跃长度。架构的详细描述见附录B.1。与（Oord et al., 2017; Razavi et al., 2019）中的VQ-VAE相比，我们进行了一些修改，如下所述。

items.en: VQ-VAEs are known to suffer from codebook collapse, wherein all encodings get mapped to a single or few embedding vectors while the other embedding vectors in the codebook are not used, reducing the information capacity of the bottleneck. To prevent this, we use random restarts: when the mean usage of a codebook vector falls below a threshold, we randomly reset it to one of the encoder outputs from the current batch. This ensures all vectors in the codebook are being used and thus have a gradient to learn from, mitigating codebook collapse.
items.zh: VQ-VAE已知存在码本崩溃问题，即所有编码被映射到单个或少数几个嵌入向量，而码本中的其他嵌入向量未被使用，从而降低了瓶颈的信息容量。为了防止这种情况，我们使用随机重启：当码本向量的平均使用率低于阈值时，我们将其随机重置为当前批次中的一个编码器输出。这确保了码本中的所有向量都被使用，从而有梯度可学习，缓解了码本崩溃。

items.en: When using the hierarchical VQ-VAE from (Razavi et al., 2019) for raw audio, we observed that the bottlenecked top level is utilized very little and sometimes experiences a complete collapse, as the model decides to pass all information through the less bottlenecked lower levels. To maximize the amount of information stored at each level, we simply train separate autoencoders with varying hop lengths. Discrete codes from each level can be treated as independent encodings of the input at different levels of compression.
items.zh: 当使用来自（Razavi et al., 2019）的分层VQ-VAE处理原始音频时，我们观察到瓶颈化的顶层利用率很低，有时会完全崩溃，因为模型决定通过瓶颈较少的较低层传递所有信息。为了最大化每个级别存储的信息量，我们简单地训练具有不同跳跃长度的独立自编码器。每个级别的离散编码可以被视为输入在不同压缩级别下的独立编码。

items.en: When using only the sample-level reconstruction loss, the model learns to reconstruct low frequencies only. To capture mid-to-high frequencies, we add a spectral loss which is defined as
items.zh: 当仅使用样本级重建损失时，模型只学习重建低频。为了捕捉中高频，我们添加了一个频谱损失，定义为

items.en: It encourages the model to match the spectral components without paying attention to phase which is more difficult to learn. This is similar to the use of power loss (Oord et al., 2018) and spectral convergence (Arık et al., 2018b) when training parallel decoders for raw audio. One difference between the latter approach and ours is that we are no longer optimizing the spectral signal-to-noise ratio; dividing by the magnitude of the signal results in numerical instability for mostly silent inputs. To prevent the model from overfitting to a particular choice of the STFT parameters, we use the sum of the spectral losses ℒspecsubscriptℒspec\mathcal{L}_{\text{spec}} calculated over multiple STFT parameters that trade-off time and frequency resolutions (Yamamoto et al., 2020).
items.zh: 它鼓励模型匹配频谱分量，而不关注相位（相位更难学习）。这类似于在训练原始音频并行解码器时使用功率损失（Oord et al., 2018）和频谱收敛（Arık et al., 2018b）。后一种方法与我们的方法的一个区别是，我们不再优化频谱信噪比；除以信号幅度会导致大部分静音输入出现数值不稳定。为了防止模型过拟合到特定的STFT参数选择，我们使用多个STFT参数（在时间和频率分辨率之间权衡）计算的频谱损失ℒ_spec之和（Yamamoto et al., 2020）。

items.en: After training the VQ-VAE, we need to learn a prior p​(𝐳)𝑝𝐳p(\mathbf{z}) over the compressed space to generate samples. We break up the prior model as
items.zh: 在训练VQ-VAE之后，我们需要学习压缩空间上的先验p(𝐳)以生成样本。我们将先验模型分解为

items.en: and train separate models for the top-level prior p​(𝐳top)𝑝superscript𝐳topp(\mathbf{z}^{\text{top}}), and upsamplers p​(𝐳middle|𝐳top)𝑝conditionalsuperscript𝐳middlesuperscript𝐳topp(\mathbf{z}^{\text{middle}}|\mathbf{z}^{\text{top}}) and p​(𝐳bottom|𝐳middle,𝐳top)𝑝conditionalsuperscript𝐳bottomsuperscript𝐳middlesuperscript𝐳topp(\mathbf{z}^{\text{bottom}}|\mathbf{z}^{\text{middle}},\mathbf{z}^{\text{top}}). Each of these is an autoregressive modeling problem in the discrete token space produced by the VQ-VAE. We use Transformers with sparse attention (Vaswani et al., 2017; Child et al., 2019) as they are currently the SOTA in autoregressive modeling. We propose a simplified version which we call the Scalable Transformer, that is easier to implement and scale (see Appendix A for details).
items.zh: 并训练顶层先验p(𝐳^{top})以及上采样器p(𝐳^{middle}|𝐳^{top})和p(𝐳^{bottom}|𝐳^{middle},𝐳^{top})的独立模型。每个都是在VQ-VAE产生的离散令牌空间中的自回归建模问题。我们使用具有稀疏注意力的Transformer（Vaswani et al., 2017; Child et al., 2019），因为它们是当前自回归建模的最先进技术。我们提出了一个简化版本，称为可扩展Transformer，它更容易实现和扩展（详见附录A）。

items.en: For the upsamplers, we need to provide the autoregressive Transformers with conditioning information from the codes of the upper levels. To do so, we use a deep residual WaveNet (Xie et al., 2017) followed by an upsampling strided convolution and a layer norm (Ba et al., 2016), and add the output as extra positional information to the embeddings of the current level. We condition the lower levels only on the chunk of upper level codes that correspond to the same segment of raw audio.
items.zh: 对于上采样器，我们需要为自回归Transformer提供来自上层编码的条件信息。为此，我们使用一个深度残差WaveNet（Xie et al., 2017），后跟一个上采样步长卷积和一个层归一化（Ba et al., 2016），并将输出作为额外的位置信息添加到当前层的嵌入中。我们仅将较低层条件化在与相同原始音频段对应的上层编码块上。

items.en: At each level, we use Transformers over the same context length of discrete codes, which correspond to increasing the raw audio length with larger hop lengths, and modeling longer temporal dependencies at the higher levels while keeping the same computational footprint for training each level. As our VQ-VAE is convolutional, we can use the same VQ-VAE to produce codes for arbitrary lengths of audio.
items.zh: 在每个级别，我们在相同上下文长度的离散编码上使用Transformer，这对应于随着跳跃长度增大而增加的原始音频长度，并在较高级别建模更长的时间依赖性，同时保持每个级别训练的计算开销相同。由于我们的VQ-VAE是卷积的，我们可以使用相同的VQ-VAE为任意长度的音频生成编码。

items.capEn: (a) Ancestral sampling: Priors for the VQ-VAE codes are trained using a cascade of Transformer models, shown in blue. Each model takes conditioning information such as genre, artist, timing, and lyrics, and the upsampler models are also conditioned on the codes from the upper levels. To generate music, the VQ-VAE codes are sampled from top to bottom using the conditioning information for control, after which the VQ-VAE decoder can convert the bottom-level codes to audio.
items.capZh: (a) 祖先采样：VQ-VAE编码的先验使用级联的Transformer模型进行训练，以蓝色显示。每个模型接受条件信息，如流派、艺术家、时间和歌词，并且上采样模型还以来自上层的编码为条件。为了生成音乐，VQ-VAE编码从上到下使用条件信息进行采样以进行控制，之后VQ-VAE解码器可以将底层编码转换为音频。

items.capEn: (b) Windowed sampling: To generate music longer than the model’s context length (12 in this figure), we repeatedly sample continuations at each level, using overlapping windows of previous codes as the context. The overlap amount is a hyperparameter, and the figure shows an example of 75% overlap with hop length 3.
items.capZh: (b) 窗口采样：为了生成比模型上下文长度更长的音乐（此图中为12），我们在每个层级重复采样延续部分，使用先前编码的重叠窗口作为上下文。重叠量是一个超参数，图中展示了75%重叠、跳跃长度为3的示例。

items.capEn: (c) Primed sampling: The model can generate continuations of an existing audio signal by converting it into the VQ-VAE codes and sampling the subsequent codes in each level.
items.capZh: (c) 引导采样：模型可以通过将现有音频信号转换为VQ-VAE编码并在每个层级采样后续编码，来生成该音频信号的延续。

full.secZh: 4.1 艺术家、流派和时序条件

items.en: Our generative model can be made more controllable by providing additional conditioning signals while training. For our first models, we provide artist and genre labels for the songs. This has two advantages: first, it reduces the entropy of the audio prediction, so the model is able to achieve better quality in any particular style. Second, at generation time, we are able to steer the model to generate in a style of our choosing. Additionally, we attach a timing signal for each segment at training time. This signal includes the total duration of the piece, the start time of that particular sample and how much fraction of the song that has elapsed. This allows the model to learn audio patterns that depend on the overall structure, such as spoken or instrumental introductions and applause at the end of a piece.
items.zh: 通过在训练时提供额外的条件信号，我们的生成模型可以变得更具可控性。对于我们的首批模型，我们为歌曲提供了艺术家和流派标签。这有两个优点：首先，它降低了音频预测的熵，因此模型能够在任何特定风格下实现更好的质量。其次，在生成时，我们能够引导模型以我们选择的风格进行生成。此外，我们在训练时为每个片段附加了一个时间信号。该信号包括作品的总时长、该特定样本的开始时间以及歌曲已播放的时间比例。这使得模型能够学习依赖于整体结构的音频模式，例如口语或器乐介绍以及作品结尾的掌声。

items.en: While the conditioned models above are able to generate songs of diverse genres and artistic styles, singing voices generated by those models, while often sung in a compelling melody, are mostly composed of babbling, rarely producing recognizable English words. In order to be able to control the generative model with lyrics, we provide more context at training time by conditioning the model on the lyrics corresponding to each audio segment, allowing the model to produce singing simultaneosly with the music.
items.zh: 尽管上述条件模型能够生成多种流派和艺术风格的歌曲，但这些模型生成的歌声虽然通常以引人入胜的旋律演唱，但大多由含糊不清的语音组成，很少产生可识别的英语单词。为了能够用歌词控制生成模型，我们在训练时通过将模型条件设置为每个音频片段对应的歌词来提供更多上下文，使模型能够同时生成歌声和音乐。

items.en: Lyrics-to-singing (LTS) task: The conditioning signal only includes the text of the lyrics, without timing or vocalisation information. We thus have to model the temporal alignment of lyrics and singing, the artists voice and also the diversity of ways one can sing a phrase depending on the pitch, melody, rhythm and even genre of the song. The conditioning data isn’t precise as the lyrics data often contains textual references to repeated sections like “chorus” or mismatching portions of lyrics with the corresponding music. There is also no separation between lead vocals, accompanying vocals and the background music in target audio. This makes the Lyrics-to-singing (LTS) task significantly more challenging than the corresponding Text-to-speech (TTS) task.
items.zh: 歌词到歌声（LTS）任务：条件信号仅包含歌词文本，没有时间或发声信息。因此，我们必须对歌词与歌声的时间对齐、艺术家的声音以及根据音高、旋律、节奏甚至歌曲流派演唱短语的多样性进行建模。条件数据并不精确，因为歌词数据通常包含对重复部分（如“合唱”）的文本引用，或者歌词与对应音乐不匹配的部分。目标音频中也没有主唱、伴唱和背景音乐之间的分离。这使得歌词到歌声（LTS）任务比相应的文本到语音（TTS）任务更具挑战性。

items.en: Providing lyrics for chunks of audio: Our dataset includes song-level lyrics, but to make the task easier we train on shorter (24 sec) chunks of audio. To provide the lyrics corresponding to the audio during training, we began with a simple heuristics of aligning the characters of the lyrics to linearly span the duration of each song, and pass a fixed-side window of characters centered around the current segment during training. While this simple strategy of linear alignment worked surprisingly well, we found that it fails for certain genres such as hip-hop with fast lyrics. To address this, we use Spleeter (Hennequin et al., 2019) to extract vocals from each song and run NUS AutoLyricsAlign (Gupta et al., 2020) on the extracted vocals to obtain a word-level alignments of the lyrics, allowing us to more accurately provide the lyrics for a given chunk of audio. We choose a large enough window so that the actual lyrics have a high probability of being inside the window.
items.zh: 为音频块提供歌词：我们的数据集包含歌曲级别的歌词，但为了简化任务，我们在较短的（24秒）音频块上进行训练。为了在训练期间提供与音频对应的歌词，我们首先采用简单的启发式方法，将歌词字符线性对齐以覆盖每首歌曲的时长，并在训练期间传递以当前片段为中心的固定侧边字符窗口。虽然这种简单的线性对齐策略效果出奇地好，但我们发现它对于某些流派（如歌词快速的嘻哈音乐）会失败。为了解决这个问题，我们使用Spleeter（Hennequin等人，2019）从每首歌曲中提取人声，并对提取的人声运行NUS AutoLyricsAlign（Gupta等人，2020）以获得歌词的词级对齐，从而使我们能够更准确地为给定的音频块提供歌词。我们选择一个足够大的窗口，使得实际歌词有很大概率位于窗口内。

items.en: Encoder-decoder model: We use an encoder-decoder style model to condition on the characters of the lyrics, with the encoder producing features from the lyrics which are attended to by the decoder which produces the top level music tokens. The lyrics encoder is a Transformer with an autoregressive modeling loss for lyrics, and its last level is used as features of the lyrics. In the music decoder, we interleave a few additional layers with encoder-decoder attention where the queries from the music tokens are only allowed to attend to keys and values from the lyrics tokens. These layers attend on the activation from the last layer of the lyrics encoder (see Figure 8(c)). In Figure 3, we see that the attention pattern learned by one of these layers corresponds to the alignment between the lyrics and the singing.
items.zh: 编码器-解码器模型：我们使用编码器-解码器风格的模型来条件于歌词的字符，编码器从歌词中生成特征，解码器通过注意力机制关注这些特征并生成顶层音乐标记。歌词编码器是一个Transformer，带有歌词的自回归建模损失，其最后一层用作歌词的特征。在音乐解码器中，我们插入一些额外的层，这些层具有编码器-解码器注意力，其中来自音乐标记的查询仅允许关注来自歌词标记的键和值。这些层关注歌词编码器最后一层的激活（见图8(c)）。在图3中，我们看到其中一个层学到的注意力模式对应于歌词与歌声之间的对齐。

items.en: To reduce computation required to train the lyrics conditional model, we use a pretrained unconditional top-level prior as our decoder and introduce the lyrics encoder using model surgery (Berner et al., 2019). We initialize the output projection weights in the MLP and the attention layers of these residual blocks to zeros (Zhang et al., 2019a), so that the added layers perform the identity function at initialization. Thus, at initialization the model behaves identically as the pretrained decoder, but there is still a gradient with respect to the encoder state and parameters222The gradient also needs to break symmetry with the encoder output features, which is the case here since the weights of the input projections in the attention are not zero., allowing the model to learn to use the encoder.
items.zh: 为了减少训练歌词条件模型所需的计算量，我们使用预训练的无条件顶层先验作为解码器，并通过模型手术（Berner等人，2019）引入歌词编码器。我们将这些残差块中MLP和注意力层的输出投影权重初始化为零（Zhang等人，2019a），使得添加的层在初始化时执行恒等函数。因此，在初始化时，模型的行为与预训练的解码器完全相同，但仍然存在关于编码器状态和参数的梯度222梯度还需要打破编码器输出特征的对称性，这里由于注意力中输入投影的权重不为零，因此满足条件。，使模型能够学习使用编码器。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Lyrics-singing alignment learned by one of the encoder-decoder attention layers. The x𝑥x-axis is the position of music queries, and the y𝑦y-axis is the position of lyric keys. The positions attended to by the decoder correspond to the characters being sung.
items.capZh: 图3：编码器-解码器注意力层之一学到的歌词-歌声对齐。x轴是音乐查询的位置，y轴是歌词键的位置。解码器关注的位置对应于正在演唱的字符。

items.en: After we have trained our VQ-VAE, upsamplers, and top level priors, we can then use them to sample novel songs.
items.zh: 在训练了我们的VQ-VAE、上采样器和顶层先验之后，我们可以使用它们来采样新颖的歌曲。

items.en: Ancestral sampling: We first generate the top level codes one token at a time by the usual ancestral sampling process (see Figure 2(a)): generating the first token, then passing all previously generated tokens into the model as inputs and outputting the next token conditioned on all previous tokens. We then run our conditioning wavenet on the top level codes to produce the conditioning information for the middle level and sample ancestrally from it too, and do the same for the bottom level.
items.zh: 祖先采样：我们首先通过通常的祖先采样过程（见图2(a)）逐个生成顶层编码：生成第一个标记，然后将所有先前生成的标记作为输入传递给模型，并输出条件于所有先前标记的下一个标记。然后，我们在顶层编码上运行我们的条件WaveNet，以生成中层编码的条件信息，并同样从中进行祖先采样，对底层也进行相同操作。

items.en: Windowed sampling: To sample segments longer than the context length, we use windowed sampling, where we move ahead our sampling window by half our context and continue sampling conditioned on this half context (See Figure 2(b)). We can trade off speed for quality by using a smaller hop length here.
items.zh: 窗口采样：为了采样比上下文长度更长的片段，我们使用窗口采样，即将采样窗口向前移动一半上下文长度，并基于这一半上下文继续采样（见图2(b)）。我们可以通过使用更小的跳跃长度来权衡速度与质量。

items.en: Primed sampling: Instead of sampling the entire token sequence from the model, we can also run a forward pass of the VQ-VAE to obtain the top, middle, and bottom level codes corresponding to a segment from an actual song, as shown in Figure 2(c). We can use these as the initial tokens in our ancestral sampling process and continue sampling from these to produce novel completions of the song.
items.zh: 引导采样：我们也可以不从头采样整个标记序列，而是对VQ-VAE进行前向传播，以获得对应于实际歌曲片段的顶层、中层和底层编码，如图2(c)所示。我们可以将这些作为祖先采样过程中的初始标记，并从中继续采样以生成歌曲的新颖延续。

items.en: We scraped a new dataset of 1.2 million songs (600k of which in English), paired with the lyrics and metadata from LyricWiki (LyricWiki, ). The metadata includes artist, album, genre, and year of the release, along with common moods or playlist keywords associated with each song. We train on 32 bit, 44.1 kHz raw audio and perform data augmentation by randomly downmixing the right and left channels to produce mono channel audio.
items.zh: 我们收集了一个包含120万首歌曲的新数据集（其中60万首为英语），并配对了来自LyricWiki的歌词和元数据。元数据包括艺术家、专辑、流派和发行年份，以及与每首歌曲相关的常见情绪或播放列表关键词。我们在32位、44.1 kHz的原始音频上进行训练，并通过随机下混左右声道生成单声道音频来进行数据增强。

items.en: For the music VQ-VAE, we use 3 levels of bottlenecks compressing 44 kHz audio in dimensionality by 8x, 32x, and 128x respectively, with a codebook size of 2048 for each level. The VQ-VAE has 2 million parameters and is trained on 9-second audio clips on 256 V100 for 3 days. We used exponential moving average to update the codebook following Razavi et al. (2019). For our prior and upsampler models, we use a context of 8192 tokens of VQ-VAE codes, which corresponds to approximately 24, 6, and 1.5 seconds of raw audio at the top, middle, and bottom level, respectively. The upsamplers have one billion parameters and are trained on 128 V100s for 2 weeks, and the top-level prior has 5 billion parameters and is trained on 512 V100s for 4 weeks. We use Adam with learning rate 0.000150.000150.00015 and weight decay of 0.0020.0020.002. For lyrics conditioning, we reuse the prior and add a small encoder, after which we train the model on 512 V100s for 2 weeks. The detailed hyperparameters for our models and training are provided in Appendix B.3.
items.zh: 对于音乐VQ-VAE，我们使用3个瓶颈层级，分别将44 kHz音频的维度压缩8倍、32倍和128倍，每个层级的码本大小为2048。VQ-VAE有200万个参数，在256个V100上训练了3天，使用9秒的音频片段。我们按照Razavi等人（2019）的方法使用指数移动平均来更新码本。对于我们的先验和上采样器模型，我们使用8192个VQ-VAE编码的上下文，分别对应顶层、中层和底层约24秒、6秒和1.5秒的原始音频。上采样器有10亿个参数，在128个V100上训练了2周；顶层先验有50亿个参数，在512个V100上训练了4周。我们使用Adam优化器，学习率为0.00015，权重衰减为0.002。对于歌词条件，我们重用先验并添加一个小型编码器，之后在512个V100上训练了2周。我们模型和训练的详细超参数见附录B.3。

items.en: We trained a sequence of models with increasing sample quality. Our first model was trained on the MAESTRO dataset using 22 kHz VQ-VAE codes and relatively small prior models. We observed that this could generate high fidelity classical music samples with piano and occasional violin. We then collected a larger and more diverse dataset of songs with genre and artist labels. The same model when trained on this new dataset was able to produce diverse samples other than classical music, and demonstrated musicality and coherence over more than a minute.
items.zh: 我们训练了一系列样本质量逐步提高的模型。我们的第一个模型在MAESTRO数据集上训练，使用22 kHz的VQ-VAE编码和相对较小的先验模型。我们观察到这可以生成高保真的古典音乐样本，包含钢琴和偶尔的小提琴。然后，我们收集了一个更大、更多样化的歌曲数据集，包含流派和艺术家标签。相同的模型在这个新数据集上训练后，能够生成除古典音乐外的多样化样本，并展现出超过一分钟的音乐性和连贯性。

items.en: Despite the novelty of being able to generate generally high fidelity and coherent songs, sample quality was still limited by a number of factors. First, the use of 22 kHz sampling rate along with small upsamplers introduced noise both in the upsampling and decoding steps, which we hear as grainy texture. We improved fidelity by using 44 kHz VQ-VAE and 1B parameter upsamplers in all subsequent experiments at the expense of longer rendering time.
items.zh: 尽管能够生成通常高保真且连贯的歌曲具有新颖性，但样本质量仍受到若干因素的限制。首先，使用22 kHz采样率和小型上采样器在上采样和解码步骤中引入了噪声，我们听到的是颗粒状的纹理。我们通过在所有后续实验中使用44 kHz的VQ-VAE和10亿参数的上采样器来提高保真度，但代价是更长的渲染时间。

items.en: Second, the 1B top-level prior was not big enough to produce singing and diverse musical timbres. We first explored increasing the model size to 5 billion parameters. Larger capacity allowed better modeling of the broader distribution of songs, resulting in samples with better musicality, longer coherence and initial singing. While there is an overall qualitative improvement, the unconditional model still struggled to sing recognizable words. Training a seq2seq model with lyric conditioning and limiting the dataset only to songs primarily in English made singing both intelligible and controllable.
items.zh: 其次，10亿参数的顶层先验不足以生成歌声和多样的音乐音色。我们首先探索将模型大小增加到50亿参数。更大的容量允许更好地建模更广泛的歌曲分布，从而产生具有更好音乐性、更长连贯性和初步歌声的样本。虽然总体上有质量改进，但无条件模型仍然难以生成可识别的单词。训练一个带有歌词条件的序列到序列模型，并将数据集限制为主要是英语的歌曲，使得歌声既清晰又可控制。

items.en: The final model, which we call Jukebox, uses all these improvements. Because everyone experiences music differently, it is generally tricky and not very meaningful to evaluate samples by the mean opinion score or FID-like metrics. We manually evaluate coherence, musicality, diversity, and novelty of generated samples. The links to curated examples are embedded in text.
items.zh: 最终模型，我们称之为Jukebox，使用了所有这些改进。由于每个人对音乐的体验不同，通过平均意见得分或类似FID的指标来评估样本通常很棘手且没有太大意义。我们手动评估生成样本的连贯性、音乐性、多样性和新颖性。精选示例的链接嵌入在文本中。

items.en: Coherence: We find the samples stay very coherent musically through the context length of the top-level prior (approximately 24 seconds), and they maintain similar harmonies and textures as we slide the window to generate longer samples. However, because the top-level does not have the context of the entire song, we do not hear long term musical patterns, and we would never hear choruses or melodies that repeat.
items.zh: 连贯性：我们发现样本在顶层先验的上下文长度（约24秒）内音乐上非常连贯，并且当我们滑动窗口以生成更长的样本时，它们保持相似的和声和纹理。然而，由于顶层没有整首歌曲的上下文，我们听不到长期音乐模式，也永远不会听到重复的副歌或旋律。

items.en: The generations progress through beginnings of songs (for example applause or slow instrumental warm-ups), through sections that sound chorus-like, through instrumental interludes, and then fading or otherwise wrapping up at the end. The top-level prior always knows what fraction of the song is complete time-wise, so it is able to imitate appropriate beginnings, middles and ends.
items.zh: 生成过程从歌曲的开头（例如掌声或缓慢的器乐热身）开始，经过类似副歌的部分、器乐间奏，然后在结尾淡出或以其他方式收尾。顶层先验始终知道歌曲在时间上完成了多少比例，因此能够模仿合适的开头、中间和结尾。

items.en: Musicality: The samples frequently imitate familiar musical harmonies and the lyrics are usually set in ways that are very natural. Frequently the highest or longest notes of the melody match words that a human singer would choose to emphasize, and the lyrics are almost always rendered in ways that capture the prosody of the phrases. This is noticeable in hip hop generations, where the model reliably captures the rhythm of spoken text. We do find that the generated melodies are usually less interesting than human composed melodies. In particular, we do not hear the antecedent and consequent pattern familiar to many human melodies, and we rarely hear choruses that are melodically memorable.
items.zh: 音乐性：样本经常模仿熟悉的音乐和声，歌词通常以非常自然的方式设置。旋律中最高或最长的音符通常与人类歌手会选择强调的词语相匹配，并且歌词几乎总是以捕捉短语韵律的方式呈现。这在嘻哈生成中尤为明显，模型可靠地捕捉了口语文本的节奏。但我们发现生成的旋律通常不如人类创作的旋律有趣。特别是，我们没有听到许多人类旋律中熟悉的起承转合模式，也很少听到旋律上令人难忘的副歌。

items.en: Diversity: Likelihood training encourages covering of all modes, so we expect the model to produce diverse samples.
items.zh: 多样性：似然训练鼓励覆盖所有模式，因此我们期望模型生成多样化的样本。

items.en: – Re-renditions: We generate multiple samples conditioned on artist and lyrics combinations that exist in our training data. While occasionally drum and bass lines or melodic intervals echo the original versions, we find that none of the generated samples is noticeably similar to the original songs.
items.zh: – 重新演绎：我们基于训练数据中存在的艺术家和歌词组合生成多个样本。虽然偶尔鼓和贝斯线或旋律音程会与原版相似，但我们发现没有一个生成的样本与原版歌曲明显相似。

items.en: We also generate multiple songs conditioned on the same artist and lyrics as Sample 1 to obtain Samples 9–12. All five sound interesting in their own ways with different moods and melodies with Sample 10 playing a harmonic at 00:14 as part of a blues riff, showing that the model has learned a wide range of singing and playing styles.
items.zh: 我们还基于与样本1相同的艺术家和歌词生成多首歌曲，得到样本9-12。所有五首歌曲都以各自的方式听起来很有趣，具有不同的情绪和旋律，其中样本10在00:14处演奏了一个布鲁斯即兴重复段中的泛音，表明模型已经学习了广泛的演唱和演奏风格。

items.en: – Completions: We prime the model with 12 seconds of existing songs and ask it to complete them in the same styles. When the priming samples include singing, the continuations are more likely to imitate the original tunes and rhythms. Songs primed with more generic or common intros tend to be more diverse. Even generated samples that are close to the originals early on deviate completely into new musical material after about 30 seconds.
items.zh: – 补全：我们用现有歌曲的12秒片段作为提示，要求模型以相同风格补全。当提示样本包含演唱时，续写更可能模仿原始曲调和节奏。以更通用或常见的开头作为提示的歌曲往往更多样化。即使早期接近原作的生成样本，在大约30秒后也会完全偏离到新的音乐素材。

items.en: Re-renditions and completions are interesting and diverse, but overall, there is still room for improvement in music quality compared to the original songs.
items.zh: 重新演绎和补全既有趣又多样，但总体而言，与原始歌曲相比，音乐质量仍有改进空间。

items.en: – Full tree: To understand diversity in a more systematic way, we generate multiple continuations from the same segment. We start with a one-minute sample and independently sample four times per one-minute extension. By the three minute mark, there are 16 completions. We can think of this branching tree as exploring different possibilities obtained by ancestral sampling. In the generated songs in the link, we hear diversity in singing and development even when the same initial segment is used. We note that this particular sample follows the lyrics more successfully than many. For certain genres like hip hop and rap, where linearly moving the window does not yield good lyrics alignment, the chance of obtaining plausible singing is lower.
items.zh: – 完整树：为了更系统地理解多样性，我们从同一片段生成多个续写。我们从一分钟样本开始，每延长一分钟独立采样四次。到三分钟标记时，有16个补全。我们可以将这棵分支树视为通过祖先采样探索不同可能性。在链接中的生成歌曲中，即使使用相同的初始片段，我们也能听到演唱和发展中的多样性。我们注意到，这个特定样本比许多样本更成功地遵循了歌词。对于某些类型如嘻哈和说唱，线性移动窗口无法产生良好的歌词对齐，获得合理演唱的机会较低。

items.en: Novelty: With the ability to condition on various styles, lyrics, and raw audio, we would like Jukebox to be a useful tool for both professional musicians and music enthusiasts alike. In this section, we are interested in exploring capabilities and applications of Jukebox.
items.zh: 新颖性：凭借对各种风格、歌词和原始音频进行条件控制的能力，我们希望Jukebox成为专业音乐家和音乐爱好者的有用工具。在本节中，我们感兴趣的是探索Jukebox的能力和应用。

items.en: – Novel styles: We generate songs in an unusual genre typically not associated with an artist. In general, we find that it is fairly difficult to generalize to a novel style of singing while using the same voice as the artist embedding overpowers other information. In Joe Bonamassa and Frank Sinatra samples, we hear a modest variation in instrumentation, energy, and ambience depending on the genre embedding. However, our attempts to mix country singer Alan Jackson with unusual genres like hip hop and punk did not seem to move the samples away from a country style in meaningful ways.
items.zh: – 新颖风格：我们生成通常与艺术家不相关的非典型流派的歌曲。总的来说，我们发现使用与艺术家嵌入相同的嗓音泛化到新颖演唱风格相当困难，因为艺术家嵌入会压倒其他信息。在Joe Bonamassa和Frank Sinatra样本中，我们根据流派嵌入听到了乐器、能量和氛围的适度变化。然而，我们尝试将乡村歌手Alan Jackson与嘻哈和朋克等非典型流派混合，并没有使样本以有意义的方式偏离乡村风格。

items.en: – Novel voices: We pick artists whose voices are reproduced reasonably well by the model, and interpolate their style embeddings to synthesize new voices. Some blending, for instance, between Frank Sinatra and Alan Jackson in Sample 4, still sounds similar to Frank Sinatra. In most cases, the model renders in a vaguely recognizable but distinct voice that preserves different vocal attributes. Samples 1 and 2 conditioned on the Céline Dion embeddings divided by two have slightly different timbre and tone but capture her unique vibrato.
items.zh: – 新颖嗓音：我们选择模型能够合理再现其嗓音的艺术家，并插值他们的风格嵌入以合成新嗓音。一些混合，例如样本4中Frank Sinatra和Alan Jackson之间的混合，听起来仍然像Frank Sinatra。在大多数情况下，模型以模糊可辨但独特的嗓音呈现，保留了不同的声音属性。样本1和2以Céline Dion嵌入除以二为条件，具有略微不同的音色和音调，但捕捉了她独特的颤音。

items.en: We also experiment with changing the style embedding in the middle of a song to create a duet (Sample 7). This is another way of guiding generation during sampling. Continuing in another voice works best when the segment ends in an interlude; otherwise, the model blends voices in the middle of a word or a sentence.
items.zh: 我们还尝试在歌曲中间更改风格嵌入以创建二重唱（样本7）。这是在采样过程中引导生成的另一种方式。当片段以间奏结束时，以另一种嗓音继续效果最佳；否则，模型会在单词或句子中间混合嗓音。

items.en: – Novel lyrics: We ask Jukebox to sing poems and novel verses generated by GPT-2 (Radford et al., ) to demonstrate that it can indeed sing new lyrics. While the training data consists of song lyrics with limited vocabulary and constrained structure, the model has learned to follow along most prompts and sing even new words that are reasonably pronounceable (including technical terms from the deep learning literature). To get the best results, however, we find that it is useful to spell out difficult words or acronyms as they are spoken. The generations are noticeably higher quality if the text matches the distribution of lyrics for the given artist, both in terms of length, and of rhyming or rhythmic qualities. For example, hip hop lyrics tend to be longer than most other genres, and the commonly emphasized syllables easily form clear rhythms.
items.zh: – 新颖歌词：我们要求Jukebox演唱由GPT-2（Radford等人，）生成的诗歌和新颖诗句，以证明它确实能演唱新歌词。虽然训练数据由词汇有限且结构受限的歌词组成，但模型已经学会了遵循大多数提示并演唱即使是合理可发音的新词（包括深度学习文献中的技术术语）。然而，为了获得最佳结果，我们发现按发音拼写难词或缩写是有用的。如果文本在长度、押韵或节奏特性上与给定艺术家的歌词分布匹配，则生成质量明显更高。例如，嘻哈歌词往往比大多数其他流派更长，并且通常强调的音节容易形成清晰的节奏。

items.en: – Novel riffs: Another useful application of Jukebox is the ability to record an incomplete idea and explore various continuations without ever needing to tabulate in symbolic representations, which would lose details of timbre and mood. We curate recordings of novel riffs by our in-house musicians and prime the model during sampling. Sample 6 starts with a musical style not widely used in Elton John’s songs. The model still carries out the tune and develops it further. Similarly, the beginning of Sample 1 is a progressive jazz piece with a 5/4 polymeter, which has never been used in hip hop. Despite this novelty, the rhythm persists throughout the song and is incorporated naturally with rapping.
items.zh: – 新颖即兴重复段：Jukebox的另一个有用应用是能够录制不完整的想法并探索各种续写，而无需以符号表示形式制表，因为这会丢失音色和情绪的细节。我们策划了内部音乐家的新颖即兴重复段录音，并在采样过程中作为模型提示。样本6以Elton John歌曲中不常用的音乐风格开始。模型仍然延续并进一步发展了曲调。类似地，样本1的开头是一首5/4复节奏的渐进爵士乐作品，这在嘻哈中从未使用过。尽管有这种新颖性，节奏在整个歌曲中持续存在，并与说唱自然融合。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Comparison of reconstructions from different VQ-VAEs, x-axis is time and y-axis is frequency. The columns from left to right are bottom-, middle-, and top-level reconstructions at hop lengths 8, 32, and 128 respectively, visualized as Mel spectrograms. The first row is the ground-truth, and the second row shows the spectrograms of audio outputs from our VQ-VAE. In the third row, we remove the spectral loss, and see that the middle and top level lose high-frequency information. In the fourth row, we use a hierarchical VQ-VAE (Razavi et al., 2019) instead of separate auto-encoders (Figure 1), and we see the middle and top levels are not used for encoding pertinent information. Finally, the fifth row shows a baseline with the Opus codec that encodes audio at constant bitrates comparable to our VQ-VAE. It also fails to capture higher frequencies and adds noticeable artifacts at the highest level of compression.
items.capZh: 图4：不同VQ-VAE重建的比较，x轴为时间，y轴为频率。从左到右的列分别是跳数为8、32和128的底层、中层和顶层重建，以梅尔频谱图可视化。第一行是真实值，第二行显示我们VQ-VAE的音频输出频谱图。第三行中，我们移除频谱损失，看到中层和顶层丢失了高频信息。第四行中，我们使用分层VQ-VAE（Razavi等人，2019）代替单独的自编码器（图1），看到中层和顶层未用于编码相关信息。最后，第五行显示以与我们VQ-VAE相当的恒定比特率编码音频的Opus编解码器基线。它也未能捕获更高频率，并在最高压缩级别添加了明显伪影。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Entropy of codebook with 2048 codes, i.e 11 bits, over training. Reviving dead codes near random encoder outputs ensures good codebook utilization from the start of training.
items.capZh: 图5：训练过程中包含2048个码（即11位）的码本熵。在随机编码器输出附近复活死码确保了从训练开始就具有良好的码本利用率。

items.en: We compare raw audio VQ-VAEs when trained with varying compression ratios, objectives, and architectures. As we use nonautoregressive decoders with continuous representation for output, we report spectral convergence (Sturmel & Daudet, 2011), which measures the amount of spectral error relative to signal, as test error and proxy for reconstruction fidelity. We evaluate on 5000 held-out 3-second audio segments and report the average in decibels. All models in this section are trained with a batch size of 32, 3-second audio clips sampled at 44 kHz. As before, we use hop lengths of 8, 32, and 128 for the bottom, middle and top level respectively.
items.zh: 我们比较了在不同压缩比、目标和架构下训练的原始音频VQ-VAE。由于我们使用具有连续表示的非自回归解码器进行输出，我们报告频谱收敛（Sturmel & Daudet, 2011），它测量相对于信号的频谱误差量，作为测试误差和重建保真度的代理。我们在5000个保留的3秒音频片段上进行评估，并以分贝报告平均值。本节中的所有模型均以批量大小32、44 kHz采样的3秒音频片段进行训练。如前所述，我们分别对底层、中层和顶层使用跳数8、32和128。

items.en: In Table 1, we see that increasing the hop size results in higher reconstruction error. Figure 4 indeed shows that a significant amount of information, especially higher frequencies, is missing at middle and top levels across all ablations we ran. This is expected as audio is compressed more with larger hop sizes. To mitigate codebook collapse, we restart dead codes near random encoder embeddings. In Figure 5, we see that this yields higher codebook usage even from early on in training. Models trained without random restarts can converge to the same test error and codebook usage but require more training steps. With poor initialization, these models sometimes end up with suboptimal codes hurting reconstruction fidelity.
items.zh: 在表1中，我们看到增加跳数会导致更高的重建误差。图4确实显示，在我们运行的所有消融实验中，中层和顶层缺失了大量信息，尤其是更高频率。这是预期的，因为随着跳数增大，音频被压缩得更多。为了缓解码本崩溃，我们在随机编码器嵌入附近重启死码。在图5中，我们看到即使在训练早期，这也产生了更高的码本使用率。没有随机重启训练的模型可以收敛到相同的测试误差和码本使用率，但需要更多训练步骤。在初始化不佳的情况下，这些模型有时会以次优码结束，损害重建保真度。

items.en: Codebook size also matters, as it sets a limit on channel capacity through the bottleneck layers. In Table 2, we find that reconstruction error increases considerably when the codebook size is reduced from 2048 to 256. We also compare with a model that uses continuous representations without vector quantization. We can think of this model as using a vastly large codebook with all encoder embeddings. This achieves almost perfect reconstruction with negligible spectral error.
items.zh: 码本大小也很重要，因为它通过瓶颈层设置了通道容量的限制。在表2中，我们发现当码本大小从2048减少到256时，重建误差显著增加。我们还与使用无向量量化的连续表示的模型进行了比较。我们可以将此模型视为使用包含所有编码器嵌入的巨大码本。这实现了几乎完美的重建，频谱误差可忽略不计。

items.en: When the model is trained with L2 loss only, reconstructions tend to sound muddy from missing high frequencies, and this problem is exacerbated as hop size is increased. In Figure 4, we see that top-level codes trained without spectral loss do not capture much information beyond 2 kHz, and obtain worse reconstructions (Table 3). However, we observe that while spectral loss helps encode more information, it also adds distortion artifacts which we hear as scratchy noise.
items.zh: 当模型仅使用L2损失训练时，重建往往因缺失高频而听起来模糊，并且随着跳数增加，这个问题更加严重。在图4中，我们看到没有频谱损失训练的顶层码在2 kHz以上捕获的信息很少，并获得更差的重建（表3）。然而，我们观察到，虽然频谱损失有助于编码更多信息，但它也增加了失真伪影，我们听到的是刺耳的噪声。

items.en: Lastly, we train a raw audio hierarchical VQ-VAE (Razavi et al., 2019) and find that it is generally difficult to push information to higher levels. This model is trained twice as long as the previous models, but middle and top-level reconstructions as shown in Figure 4 are not capturing much. It is possible that higher level codes may have collapsed before bottom level starts to reconstruct the audio well. Making the bottom layers explicitly model residuals pushed more information to the top. But, we found separate autoencoders to be cleaner and more effective.
items.zh: 最后，我们训练了一个原始音频分层VQ-VAE（Razavi等人，2019），发现通常很难将信息推送到更高层级。该模型的训练时间是之前模型的两倍，但如图4所示，中间和顶层重建并没有捕捉到太多信息。有可能在底层开始良好重建音频之前，高层级代码已经崩溃。让底层显式建模残差将更多信息推向了顶层。但我们发现单独的自编码器更简洁有效。

items.en: Generative modeling in deep learning: Generative models aim to learn the distribution of data by either explicitly by modeling the distribution or implicitly by constructing means to sample from it (Goodfellow, 2016). Modeling the interdependency within high-dimensional data was traditionally considered extremely difficult, but starting with Deep Boltzmann Machines (Salakhutdinov & Hinton, 2009), various kinds of deep generative models have been introduced. Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) (Goodfellow et al., 2014) use generator and discriminator networks that contest each other to make the generated samples as indistinguishable as possible from the data, and they are renowned for their ability to generate high-quality pictures (Zhang et al., 2019b; Brock et al., 2019). Autoregressive generative models such as NADE (Uria et al., 2016), PixelCNN (Van den Oord et al., 2016), and Transformers (Vaswani et al., 2017) use the chain rule of probability to factorize the joint distribution of data into a product of simpler distributions, and flow-based models (Dinh et al., 2015, 2017; Rezende & Mohamed, 2015; Kingma & Dhariwal, 2018) learn a series of invertible transformations that maps the data distribution with a simpler one such as a Gaussian distribution. Autoregressive flows (Papamakarios et al., 2017; Kingma et al., 2016) combine the two ideas to achieve faster density estimation or data generation. Variational autoencoders (VAEs) (Rezende et al., 2014; Kingma & Welling, 2014) impose a Gaussian prior on the latent code in an encoder-decoder setup from which data can be sampled.
items.zh: 深度学习中的生成建模：生成模型旨在通过学习数据分布（显式建模分布）或通过构建从中采样的方法（隐式）来学习数据分布（Goodfellow，2016）。对高维数据内部依赖关系进行建模传统上被认为极其困难，但从深度玻尔兹曼机（Salakhutdinov & Hinton，2009）开始，各种深度生成模型被引入。生成对抗网络（GAN）（Goodfellow等人，2014）使用生成器和判别器网络相互对抗，使生成的样本与数据尽可能无法区分，以其生成高质量图像的能力而闻名（Zhang等人，2019b；Brock等人，2019）。自回归生成模型如NADE（Uria等人，2016）、PixelCNN（Van den Oord等人，2016）和Transformer（Vaswani等人，2017）使用概率链式法则将数据的联合分布分解为更简单分布的乘积；基于流的模型（Dinh等人，2015，2017；Rezende & Mohamed，2015；Kingma & Dhariwal，2018）学习一系列可逆变换，将数据分布映射为更简单的分布（如高斯分布）。自回归流（Papamakarios等人，2017；Kingma等人，2016）结合了这两种思想，以实现更快的密度估计或数据生成。变分自编码器（VAE）（Rezende等人，2014；Kingma & Welling，2014）在编码器-解码器设置中对潜在代码施加高斯先验，从而可以从中采样数据。

items.en: Generative models for music: Generative modeling of symbolic music dates back to more than half a century, when Hiller Jr & Isaacson (1957) introduced the first computer-generated music based on Markov chains. There exists a variety of earlier approaches using rule-based systems (Moorer, 1972), chaos and self-similarity (Pressing, 1988), cellular automata (Beyls, 1989), concatenative synthesis (Jehan, 2005), and constraint programming (Anders & Miranda, 2011). More recent data-driven approaches include DeepBach (Hadjeres et al., 2017) and Coconet (Huang et al., 2017) which use Gibbs sampling to produce notes in the style of Bach chorals, MidiNet (Yang et al., 2017) and MuseGAN (Dong et al., 2018) which use generative adversarial networks, MusicVAE (Roberts et al., 2018) and HRNN (Wu et al., 2019) which use hierarchical recurrent networks, and Music Transformer (Huang et al., 2019a) and MuseNet (Payne, 2019) which use Transformers to autoregressively predict MIDI note events. There also have been a number of approaches for synthesizing music conditioned on symbolic music information, such as NSynth (Engel et al., 2017) which uses WaveNet-style autoencoder, Mel2Mel (Kim et al., 2019) and Wave2Midi2Wave (Hawthorne et al., 2019) which synthesize music using WaveNet conditioned on a piano roll representation, and GanSynth (Engel et al., 2019) which uses generative adversarial networks to produce magnitude spectrograms together with instananeous frequencies for easier spectrogram inversion. Generative models for music can also be used for music style transfer, as seen in Midi-VAE (Brunner et al., 2018) which uses a variational autoencoder to transfer styles between classical and jazz music, LakhNES (Donahue et al., 2019) which uses a Transformer architecture to generate chiptune music, and Universal Music Translator Network (Mor et al., 2019) which uses a denoising autoencoder that can disentangle musical style and content.
items.zh: 音乐生成模型：符号音乐的生成建模可以追溯到半个多世纪前，当时Hiller Jr & Isaacson（1957）引入了第一个基于马尔可夫链的计算机生成音乐。存在多种早期方法，包括基于规则的系统（Moorer，1972）、混沌和自相似性（Pressing，1988）、元胞自动机（Beyls，1989）、拼接合成（Jehan，2005）和约束编程（Anders & Miranda，2011）。最近的数据驱动方法包括DeepBach（Hadjeres等人，2017）和Coconet（Huang等人，2017），它们使用吉布斯采样生成巴赫合唱风格的音符；MidiNet（Yang等人，2017）和MuseGAN（Dong等人，2018）使用生成对抗网络；MusicVAE（Roberts等人，2018）和HRNN（Wu等人，2019）使用分层循环网络；Music Transformer（Huang等人，2019a）和MuseNet（Payne，2019）使用Transformer自回归预测MIDI音符事件。还有多种方法用于根据符号音乐信息合成音乐，例如NSynth（Engel等人，2017）使用WaveNet风格的自编码器；Mel2Mel（Kim等人，2019）和Wave2Midi2Wave（Hawthorne等人，2019）使用以钢琴卷帘表示为条件的WaveNet合成音乐；GanSynth（Engel等人，2019）使用生成对抗网络生成幅度谱图以及瞬时频率，以便更容易进行谱图反演。音乐生成模型也可用于音乐风格迁移，如Midi-VAE（Brunner等人，2018）使用变分自编码器在古典和爵士音乐之间迁移风格；LakhNES（Donahue等人，2019）使用Transformer架构生成芯片音乐；Universal Music Translator Network（Mor等人，2019）使用去噪自编码器，可以解耦音乐风格和内容。

items.en: Sample-level generation of audio: In recent years, a variety of generative models for raw audio have been introduced. WaveNet (Oord et al., 2016) performs autoregressive sample-by-sample probabilistic modeling of raw waveform using a series of dilated convolutions to exponentially increase the context length. It can produce realistic audio either unconditionally or by conditioning on acoustic features or spectrograms. The autoregressive nature of WaveNet makes the sampling notoriously slow, and it uses a categorical distribution for audio samples which introduces quantization noise. Parallel WaveNet (Oord et al., 2018) improves upon this by instead using a mixture of logistics distribution, a continuous probability distribution, and performing probability density distillation which learns a parallel feed-forward network from a pre-trained autoregressive model, allowing faster sampling of high fidelity audio. ClariNet (Ping et al., 2019) achieves similar audio quality using a simple Gaussian distribution instead and thus having a closed-form loss function, eliminating the need for Monte-Carlo sampling. SampleRNN (Mehri et al., 2017) uses a multi-scale, hierarchical recurrent neural network with convolutional upsampling to model long-range complex structures. WaveRNN (Kalchbrenner et al., 2018) uses recurrent neural networks that operate separately on the most significant and the least significant bytes, which can be efficiently deployed in mobile devices while having comparable audio quality to WaveNet. WaveGlow (Prenger et al., 2019) is a flow-based model for parallel sample-level audio synthesis, which can be trained with a straightforward maximum-likelihood estimation and thus is advantageous to the two-stage training process needed for distillation. Parallel WaveGAN (Yamamoto et al., 2020) and MelGAN (Kumar et al., 2019) are GAN-based approaches directly modeling audio waveforms, achieving similar quality as WaveNet and WaveGlow models with significantly fewer parameters. While the approaches above serve as sophisticated generative models for raw audio to be conditioned on a compact and controllable representation of audio such as Mel spectrograms, MelNet (Vasquez & Lewis, 2019) takes a different approach of hierarchically generating accurate high-resolution Mel spectrograms, after which a simple gradient-based optimization can produce high-fidelity audio.
items.zh: 音频样本级生成：近年来，出现了多种原始音频生成模型。WaveNet（Oord等人，2016）使用一系列扩张卷积指数级增加上下文长度，对原始波形进行自回归逐样本概率建模。它可以无条件或通过以声学特征或谱图为条件生成逼真的音频。WaveNet的自回归性质使得采样速度极慢，并且它对音频样本使用分类分布，引入了量化噪声。Parallel WaveNet（Oord等人，2018）通过使用混合逻辑分布（一种连续概率分布）并进行概率密度蒸馏来改进，该蒸馏从预训练的自回归模型中学习并行前馈网络，从而允许更快地采样高保真音频。ClariNet（Ping等人，2019）使用简单的高斯分布代替，从而具有闭式损失函数，消除了蒙特卡洛采样的需要，实现了类似的音频质量。SampleRNN（Mehri等人，2017）使用多尺度分层循环神经网络和卷积上采样来建模长程复杂结构。WaveRNN（Kalchbrenner等人，2018）使用循环神经网络分别处理最高有效字节和最低有效字节，可以在移动设备上高效部署，同时具有与WaveNet相当的音频质量。WaveGlow（Prenger等人，2019）是一种基于流的并行样本级音频合成模型，可以通过直接的最大似然估计进行训练，因此相对于蒸馏所需的两阶段训练过程具有优势。Parallel WaveGAN（Yamamoto等人，2020）和MelGAN（Kumar等人，2019）是基于GAN的方法，直接建模音频波形，以显著更少的参数实现了与WaveNet和WaveGlow模型相似的质量。虽然上述方法作为复杂的原始音频生成模型，以紧凑且可控的音频表示（如梅尔谱图）为条件，但MelNet（Vasquez & Lewis，2019）采用了一种不同的方法，分层生成精确的高分辨率梅尔谱图，之后通过简单的基于梯度的优化即可生成高保真音频。

items.en: VQ-VAE: Oord et al. (2017) introduced VQ-VAE, an approach of downsampling extremely long context inputs to a shorter-length discrete latent encoding using a vector quantization, and they showed that it can generate both high-quality images and audio, as well as learn unsupervized representations of phonemes. Razavi et al. (2019) extended the above model by introducing a hierarchy of discrete representations for images and showed that the resulting model can learn to separate high-level semantics into the highest level of discrete codes which have the largest receptive field, while capturing local features like textures in the lower levels with smaller receptive fields. They used the hierarchical model to generate high-diversity and high-fidelity images for the conditional ImageNet and FFHQ datasets. Dieleman et al. (2018) tried variants of this approach where instead of a single encoder there are successive encoders that each further compress the lossy discrete encodings from the previous levels. A downside of this approach is that information is lost at each step and requires separate training for each VQ-VAE level, and it leads to a hierarchy collapse problem. De Fauw et al. (2019) used AR decoders which are known to cause the problem of ignoring the latent variables, and they suggested ways to mitigate it. The feed-forward decoders from (Razavi et al., 2019) do not suffer from this issue, and thus we use their approach.
items.zh: VQ-VAE：Oord等人（2017）引入了VQ-VAE，一种使用向量量化将极长上下文输入下采样为较短长度的离散潜在编码的方法，并表明它可以生成高质量图像和音频，以及学习音素的无监督表示。Razavi等人（2019）通过引入图像离散表示的分层结构扩展了上述模型，并表明所得模型可以学习将高层语义分离到具有最大感受野的最高层离散代码中，同时将局部特征（如纹理）捕获到具有较小感受野的较低层中。他们使用分层模型为条件ImageNet和FFHQ数据集生成高多样性和高保真图像。Dieleman等人（2018）尝试了该方法的变体，其中使用连续的编码器，每个编码器进一步压缩来自前一层的损失性离散编码。这种方法的一个缺点是每一步都会丢失信息，并且需要为每个VQ-VAE层级单独训练，导致层级崩溃问题。De Fauw等人（2019）使用了自回归解码器，已知这会导致忽略潜在变量的问题，并提出了缓解方法。（Razavi等人，2019）的前馈解码器不存在此问题，因此我们采用他们的方法。

items.en: Speech synthesis: Producing natural human voice entails an understanding of linguistic features, mapping of sounds, and steerability of expression. Many text-to-speech (TTS) systems rely on highly engineered features (Klatt, 1980), carefully curated sound segments (Hunt & Black, 1996), statistical parametric modeling (Zen et al., 2009), and often complex pipelines as described in (Arık et al., 2017). These approaches are fairly involved and produce unnatural or inarticulate voices. More recent works like Deep Voice 3 (Ping et al., 2018), Tacotron 2 (Shen et al., 2018), and Char2Wav (Sotelo et al., 2017) learn speech synthesis end-to-end using sequence-to-sequence architecture (Sutskever et al., 2014). The design space is vast, but in general, typical approaches comprise of a bidirectional encoder, a decoder, and a vocoder to build text representations, audio features, and the final raw waveforms. To generate multiple voices, text-to-speech models can also condition on the speaker identity (Oord et al., 2016; Gibiansky et al., 2017; Jia et al., 2018) as well as text prompt. By learning and manipulating auxiliary embeddings, models can mimic a new voice (Arık et al., 2018a; Taigman et al., 2018) at test time. These methods, however, require labeled data. Ideas like clustering (Dehak et al., 2011), priming (Wang et al., 2018), and variational autoencoders (Hsu et al., 2019; Akuzawa et al., 2018) have been used to learn broader styles of speech and control expressivity in an unsupervised way. There are also works on synthesizing singing by additionally controlling pitch and timbre. Similar to TTS literature, early works use concatenative methods (Bonada & Serra, 2007) that join short segments of curated singing, and statistical parametric methods (Saino et al., 2006; Oura et al., 2010) which allow modeling of timbre from training data. Both approaches impose fairly strong assumptions resulting in noticeable artifacts. (Blaauw & Bonada, 2017) train a neural TTS model with a parametric vocoder to separate pitch and timbre which can be controlled at generation time.
items.zh: 语音合成：产生自然的人声需要理解语言特征、声音映射和表达的可控性。许多文本到语音（TTS）系统依赖于高度工程化的特征（Klatt，1980）、精心策划的声音片段（Hunt & Black，1996）、统计参数建模（Zen等人，2009），以及如（Arık等人，2017）所述的复杂流水线。这些方法相当复杂，且产生不自然或含糊不清的声音。最近的工作如Deep Voice 3（Ping等人，2018）、Tacotron 2（Shen等人，2018）和Char2Wav（Sotelo等人，2017）使用序列到序列架构（Sutskever等人，2014）端到端地学习语音合成。设计空间很大，但通常典型方法包括双向编码器、解码器和声码器，以构建文本表示、音频特征和最终的原始波形。为了生成多种声音，文本到语音模型还可以以说话人身份（Oord等人，2016；Gibiansky等人，2017；Jia等人，2018）以及文本提示为条件。通过学习和操作辅助嵌入，模型可以在测试时模仿新声音（Arık等人，2018a；Taigman等人，2018）。然而，这些方法需要标注数据。聚类（Dehak等人，2011）、启动（Wang等人，2018）和变分自编码器（Hsu等人，2019；Akuzawa等人，2018）等思想已被用于以无监督方式学习更广泛的语音风格并控制表现力。还有通过额外控制音高和音色来合成唱歌的工作。与TTS文献类似，早期工作使用拼接方法（Bonada & Serra，2007）连接精心策划的唱歌短片段，以及统计参数方法（Saino等人，2006；Oura等人，2010），允许从训练数据中建模音色。这两种方法都施加了相当强的假设，导致明显的伪影。（Blaauw & Bonada，2017）训练了一个带有参数声码器的神经TTS模型，以分离音高和音色，从而在生成时进行控制。

items.en: While our approach represents a step forward in the ability to generate coherent long raw audio music samples, we recognize several directions for future work. Great music generation should be high quality over all time scales: it should have a developing musical and emotional structure across the entire piece, local notes and harmonies that always make sense, nuanced and appropriate small timbral and textural details, and audio recording quality that balances and blends the multiple voices well, and without unwanted noise. We view our current model as stronger on the mid-range time scales: often the model generates samples that locally sound very good, with interesting and diverse harmonies, rhythms, instruments, and voices. We have frequently been very impressed how the melody and rhythm generated suits a particular lyric extremely well. However, while the samples stay consistent over longer time scales, we notice they don’t have traditional larger music structures (such as choruses that repeat, or melodies that have a question and answer form). Additionally, on the smallest scale, we sometimes hear audio noise or scratchiness.
items.zh: 虽然我们的方法代表了在生成连贯的长原始音频音乐样本能力上向前迈出的一步，但我们认识到未来工作的几个方向。伟大的音乐生成应在所有时间尺度上具有高质量：它应在整个乐曲中具有发展的音乐和情感结构，局部音符和和声始终合理，细微且恰当的小音色和纹理细节，以及良好平衡和融合多个声音且无不需要噪声的音频录制质量。我们认为我们当前的模型在中程时间尺度上更强：模型通常生成局部听起来非常好的样本，具有有趣且多样的和声、节奏、乐器和声音。我们经常对生成的旋律和节奏与特定歌词非常匹配印象深刻。然而，虽然样本在更长的时间尺度上保持一致，但我们注意到它们没有传统的大型音乐结构（例如重复的副歌，或具有问答形式的旋律）。此外，在最小尺度上，我们有时会听到音频噪声或刺耳声。

items.en: Beyond the quality of the samples, we also would look to diversify the languages and styles the model is able to generate. Our current model has been trained only on songs whose primary language as detected by (Sites, 2013) is English. In the future, we would look to include other languages and artists. We believe this will be of interest both for generating strictly in those styles, and because historically we have seen much creativity and development coming from unusual blends of existing musical styles.
items.zh: 除了样本质量，我们还将寻求多样化模型能够生成的语言和风格。我们当前的模型仅训练于（Sites，2013）检测为主要语言为英语的歌曲。未来，我们将考虑包括其他语言和艺术家。我们相信这将引起兴趣，既用于严格生成这些风格，也因为历史上我们看到许多创造力和发展来自现有音乐风格的不寻常融合。

items.en: Finally, we consider it very important that computer music generation also serves as a tool for human musicians, and increasingly those interested in music but without formal training. While we are able to steer our current model somewhat through lyric and midi conditioning, we can imagine many other possible ways for humans to influence the generations, including indicating the mood or dynamic at various sections, or controlling when drums, singers, or other instruments should play.
items.zh: 最后，我们认为计算机音乐生成作为人类音乐家的工具非常重要，并且越来越多地服务于对音乐感兴趣但没有正式训练的人。虽然我们目前能够通过歌词和MIDI条件在一定程度上引导模型，但我们可以想象人类影响生成的许多其他可能方式，包括指示各个部分的情緒或动态，或控制何时播放鼓、歌手或其他乐器。

items.en: The current model takes around an hour to generate 1 minute of top level tokens. The upsampling process is very slow, as it proceeds sequentially through the sample. Currently it takes around 8 hours to upsample one minute of top level tokens. We can create a human-in-the-loop co-composition process at the top level only, using the VQ-VAE decoders to get a fast upsampling of the top level tokens to hear a very rough sense of what the model generates. The top-level model generates multiple samples, the person picks a favorite (listening to the rough VQ-VAE decoding), and then the model continues generating multiple samples continuing the favorite. This process would be significantly improved with faster generation and Transformer upsampling steps. Our models have fast parallel evaluation of likelihood but slow autoregressive sampling. We can instead use a model with fast parallel sampling but slow autoregressive likelihood evaluation (Kingma et al., 2016), and distill the information from our current model into it (Oord et al., 2018). The distillation works by generating samples from the parallel sampler and evaluating it likelihood and entropy using the parallel likelihood evaluator, and then optimising the sampler by minimising the KL divergence of it from our current model.
items.zh: 当前模型生成1分钟顶层令牌大约需要1小时。上采样过程非常慢，因为它顺序遍历样本。目前，上采样1分钟顶层令牌大约需要8小时。我们可以仅在顶层创建人在环路的协同创作过程，使用VQ-VAE解码器快速上采样顶层令牌，以听到模型生成的大致感觉。顶层模型生成多个样本，人选择一个最喜欢的（通过粗略的VQ-VAE解码收听），然后模型继续生成多个样本延续最喜欢的。这个过程将通过更快的生成和Transformer上采样步骤显著改进。我们的模型具有快速的并行似然评估，但自回归采样缓慢。我们可以改用具有快速并行采样但缓慢自回归似然评估的模型（Kingma等人，2016），并将当前模型的信息蒸馏到其中（Oord等人，2018）。蒸馏通过从并行采样器生成样本并使用并行似然评估器评估其似然和熵，然后通过最小化其与当前模型的KL散度来优化采样器。

items.en: We have introduced Jukebox, a model that generates raw audio music imitating many different styles and artists. We can condition this music on specific artists and genres, and can optionally specify the lyrics for the sample. We laid out the details necessary to train a Hierarchical VQ-VAE to compress the music effectively into tokens. While previous work has generated raw audio music in the 20–30 second range, our model is capable of generating pieces that are multiple minutes long, and with recognizable singing in natural-sounding voices.
items.zh: 我们介绍了Jukebox，一个生成模仿多种不同风格和艺术家的原始音频音乐的模型。我们可以以特定艺术家和流派为条件，并可选地指定样本的歌词。我们详细说明了训练分层VQ-VAE以有效将音乐压缩为令牌所需的细节。虽然之前的工作生成了20-30秒范围内的原始音频音乐，但我们的模型能够生成数分钟长的片段，并具有可识别的自然声音演唱。

items.en: We would like to thank John Schulman and Will Guss for producing and performing novel riffs for our sampling experiments, and Rewon Child, Aditya Ramesh, Ryan Lowe and Jack Clark for providing feedback for initial drafts of this paper.
items.zh: 我们要感谢John Schulman和Will Guss为我们的采样实验创作和演奏新颖的即兴重复段，以及Rewon Child、Aditya Ramesh、Ryan Lowe和Jack Clark为本文初稿提供反馈。
